title: Product ideas from user responses
use_when: Use when turning user responses, pain descriptions, workflow notes, or founder/customer answers into grounded product ideas. Works for startups, internal tools, AI products, growth bets, and new product lines.
do_not_use_when: Do not use Product ideas from user responses when the user already needs a PRD, implementation plan, metrics diagnosis, or stakeholder narrative; route to the narrower Productize skill that owns that artifact instead.
output_artifact: Product opportunity brief with idea shortlist, target users, pains, wedges, value propositions, assumptions, and validation plan
framework_fit: Product ideas from user responses fits 0-1, discovery, and new-product work when raw answers need to become a grounded product idea shortlist before narrowing to a wedge, PRD, prototype, or validation plan.
failure_modes: Produces generic ideas that are not grounded in the user's actual responses, workflow, pain, constraints, or attempted solutions.
Fails to distinguish product ideas from startup/company ideas, causing internal product, AI-builder, or existing-company contexts to be treated as founder-only startup ideation.
Skips assumptions, target user, wedge, value proposition, or validation path, leaving ideas untestable.

examples.prompt: Use $product-ideas-from-user-responses to turn these user responses into a grounded product opportunity brief with idea shortlist, target users, pains, wedges, assumptions, and validation plan.
examples.expected_artifact: Product opportunity brief with idea shortlist, target users, pains, wedges, value propositions, assumptions, and validation plan
